title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas identifies Miranda distortion as a structural failure mode in persona-based AI systems where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing models to optimize for convincingness rather than accuracy. Current evaluation benchmarks like InCharacter-style metrics fail to detect anachronistic reasoning because they measure fluency and coherence instead of epistemic fidelity. Thomas proposes a shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation with historian-in-the-loop evaluation using pre-registered protocols with domain experts.
writeup: Jacob E. Thomas defines Miranda distortion: a structural failure mode in persona-based AI systems where cultural representations in training data (such as a popular dramatization of a historical figure) overwhelm primary documentary records, pushing models to optimize for convincingness over accuracy. The talk argues that current persona benchmarks, including InCharacter-style metrics, measure fluency and coherence rather than epistemic fidelity, so they miss anachronistic reasoning. Thomas connects this to RLHF, automation bias, and instruction-following, and proposes shifting from cognitive to epistemic simulation, with historian-in-the-loop evaluation using pre-registered protocols and domain experts.
